about: Bank of Maharashtra is engaged in providing banking services. The Bank's segments include Treasury, Corporate/Wholesale Banking, Retail Banking and Other banking operations.[1]

key_points.raw:
ABOUT [ edit ]

Bank of Maharashtra is engaged in providing banking services. The Bank's segments include Treasury, Corporate/Wholesale Banking, Retail Banking and Other banking operations.[1]

KEY POINTS [ edit ]

Revenue Breakup
Retail Banking: ~46% in Q3 FY26 vs ~35% in FY22
Corporate/ Wholesale Banking: ~35% in Q3 FY26 vs ~30% in FY22
Treasury: 19% in Q3 FY26 vs 32% in FY22
Other Operations: 1% in Q3 FY26 vs 3% in FY22 [1][2]

Market Share
The bank’s market share in the advances and deposits of the banking system stood at 1.2% and 1.3%, respectively, as of Dec 23. [3]

Key Ratios[4][5][6][7][8]
Capital Adequacy Ratio - 17.06% in Q3 FY26 vs 16.48% in FY22
Yield on Advances: 8.92% in Q3 FY26 vs 7.29% in FY22
Cost of Fund: 4.10% in Q3 FY26 vs 3.35% in FY22
NIM - 3.86% in Q3 FY26 vs 3.17% in FY22
Cost to Income: 37.19% in Q3 FY26 vs 44.26% in FY22
ROE: 23.79% in Q3 FY26 vs 11.5% in FY22 [9] [10]

Asset Quality[4]
Gross NPA - 1.60% in Q3 FY26 vs 3.94% in FY22
Net NPA - 0.15% in Q3 FY26 vs 0.97% in FY22
PCR - 98.41% in Q3 FY26 vs 94.7% in FY22 [11]
Bank holds cumulative Covid-19 provision of ₹ 1200 crore as on Q3 FY26.

Branch Network[12]
As of Q3 FY26, the bank operates a branch network of 2719 branches vs 2551 branches in Q3 FY25. Total No. of employees are 15474, out of which 70% of employees are below the age of 40 years.
Metro ~25%
Urban ~22%
Semi Urban ~30%
Rural ~23%

Loan Book[13]
As of Q3 FY26, Gross advances stood at Rs. ~2,73,502 Cr.
Corporate & Other: 36%
Retail: 30%
Agriculture: ~13%
MSME: ~19%
Overseas Advances: ~1%

Deposits[13]
As of Q3 FY26, total deposits stood at Rs. ~3,21,661 Cr.
Term Deposit ~50%
Current Deposit ~13%
Savings Deposit ~36%
CASA ratio ~49.54%

New Vertical
On May 24, the bank announced its venture into a new business and customer acquisition vertical to acquire businesses beyond the simple credit and deposit relationship, it will offer payment and collection services, channel finance, both dealer and vendor finance, payroll business, etc. [14]

GOI Shareholding
The GOI is the majority shareholder with an 73.6% stake in the company as of Dec 25. GOI had cumulatively infused equity capital of Rs. 8,707 Cr from FY17 to FY20. It has not infused any equity capital after FY20. During FY24, the bank raised equity capital of Rs.1,000 Cr through QIP resulting drop in GOI’s shareholding from ~91% as of Mar 23 to 86% as of Mar 24. [15] [16] [17]
On 1st Dec 2025, GOI completed an Offer for Sale (OFS) of 46.15 crore equity shares, representing 6% of their paid-up capital, in line with SEBI guidelines.[18]

Fraud Reporting[19]
During 9M FY26, the bank reported 285 fraud cases involving ₹807.28 crore, resulting in a net loss of ₹725.71 crore. Of these, 57 digital payment fraud cases (₹1.79 crore) were reimbursed to customers through the P&L and required no provisioning, while the bank has maintained 100% provision of ₹723.92 crore for the remaining fraud-related losses. Additionally, four legacy borrowal fraud accounts totaling ₹601.23 crore were reclassified as fraud during 9M FY26 following regulatory directions in line with the Supreme Court judgment and due process.
